item.title: Using a Python 3 LSP server with Python 2 code works (more or less) 摘要
item.description:
The author describes using Python 3 LSP servers (pylsp and ruff) to edit Python 2 code in GNU Emacs, finding it surprisingly functional. Despite Python 2 syntax differences like print statements and mixed tabs/spaces indentation, the LSP servers mostly work without excessive false errors, correctly identifying real issues like misspelled variables. Features like go-to-definition and find references function adequately. The author maintains Python 2 code for legacy work systems where migration to Python 3 is risky, and prefers using a superintelligent editor for such tasks.
标签: Python, Python 2, LSP, Emacs, code editing, legacy code, pylsp, ruff, programming tools, migration
